canonical_definition: The degree to which an unlearning method successfully removes the influence of specified data from a model, often measured by comparing the unlearned model's outputs to a model never trained on the data.
papers: Benchmarking Vision Language Model Unlearning via Fictitious Facial Identity Dataset
LLM Unlearning via Loss Adjustment with Only Forget Data

definitions.text: The degree to which an unlearning method successfully removes the influence of specified data from a model, often measured by comparing the unlearned model's outputs to a model never trained on the data.
definitions.paper: LLM Unlearning via Loss Adjustment with Only Forget Data

relationships.paper: LLM Unlearning via Loss Adjustment with Only Forget Data
relationships.evidence: To assess forget quality and model utility, we mainly use two metrics proposed alongside the TOFU dataset, Forget Quality (FQ) and Model Utility (MU) (Maini et al., 2024a). (Section 4.3)
